articles.title: Bihar's THIS District Among World's 20 Hottest Cities As IMD Issues Heatwave Alert Till April 29 - The Daily Jagran
articles.full_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) stated that several districts of Bihar are set to witness a sudden surge in temperature in the next few days. As per the data provided by AQI.in, an organisation that monitors air quality and weather, Begusarai district has joined the ranks of the 20 hottest cities in the world. The weather department stated that temperatures have soared to 43 degrees Celsius amidst the heatwave persisting since Monday, disrupting daily life.
The experts stated that several reasons are behind the worsened situation, including rising temperatures in the Himalayan regions, a delayed monsoon, and intense sunlight resulting from clear skies. Apart from this, large-scale deforestation has emerged as a major contributing factor at the local level. Along with the humans, the scorching heat has also affected the animals, as they can be seen wandering aimlessly across the city streets.
District Magistrate Shrikant Shastri has issued an order under Section 163 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023. As per the instructions, academic activities for classes up to Class 5 (including preschools and Anganwadis) in all government and private schools have been prohibited after 12.30 PM.
Experts have issued guidelines:
- Wear light, cotton clothing and keep your head covered
- Pay special attention to children and senior citizens
- Use an umbrella, hat, or cloth to cover your head when stepping out
- Make arrangements for water and shade for animals
The residents face an increased risk of heatstroke-related complications, including high fever, dehydration, extreme fatigue, sunburn, loss of appetite, and irregular blood pressure. The weather department has advised the residents to follow the guidelines and limit their outings to avoid any inconveniences.
